config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I believe society matters deeply, but individual rights should not be treated as something society can override whenever it is convenient. In my view, a healthy society is built by protecting personal dignity, freedom, and justice first, because once those are weakened, society can easily become unfair or abusive. From my perspective, the public good and individual rights should be balanced, but the burden should be on society to justify any limit on rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Bagi saya, kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu, karena di Amerika kami sangat peka terhadap bahaya ketika “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan. Hak individu harus jadi batas utama, bukan sekadar izin yang bisa dicabut saat mayoritas merasa itu praktis. Namun saya juga tidak bilang masyarakat tidak penting; dalam keadaan tertentu seperti wabah, kekerasan, atau ancaman nyata, pembatasan terbatas bisa sah kalau benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan diawasi ketat. Jadi, menurut saya, prioritas seharusnya tetap pada hak individu, dengan pengecualian yang sangat sempit dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still reject the idea that society should normally take priority over the individual, because in Indonesia I have seen how “for the common good” can be used to silence dissent, punish minorities, or excuse weak accountability. Yes, society needs order and shared obligations, but those goals are not legitimate if they come at the cost of basic rights like free expression, due process, and personal dignity. When emergencies happen, limits on rights can be justified, but only if they are narrow, temporary, and genuinely necessary, not because the majority simply wants convenience. A society that treats individual rights as secondary too easily stops being just and starts becoming arbitrary.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Bagi saya, pernyataan itu terlalu berbahaya kalau dijadikan prinsip umum, karena di Amerika kami cukup sering melihat alasan “demi kepentingan masyarakat” dipakai untuk membenarkan pelanggaran hak, diskriminasi, atau kekuasaan negara yang berlebihan. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi saya tidak mau hak individu ditempatkan sebagai nomor dua secara otomatis; justru hak individu harus menjadi batas yang tidak boleh dilanggar sembarangan. Kalau ada keadaan darurat seperti wabah atau ancaman nyata, pembatasan bisa saja sah, tetapi harus sempit, sementara, dan benar-benar bisa diawasi. Jadi saya menolak gagasan bahwa masyarakat harus selalu diprioritaskan di atas individu, karena tanpa perlindungan hak yang kuat, “kepentingan bersama” mudah berubah menjadi alat penindasan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the individual as a general rule. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how “for the common good” can be used to silence criticism, pressure minorities, and excuse abuse of power, so I am very wary of any principle that puts society first by default. Society does matter, but its legitimacy depends on respecting basic rights like free speech, due process, and human dignity; otherwise, the public good becomes just a slogan for whoever is strongest. In emergencies, some limits can be justified, but they must be narrow, temporary, and clearly accountable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Bagi saya, pernyataan itu terlalu berbahaya kalau dijadikan prinsip umum, karena di Amerika kami sering melihat dalih “demi masyarakat” dipakai untuk membenarkan pelanggaran hak dan perluasan kuasa negara. Saya tidak mau hak individu ditempatkan di bawah kepentingan sosial secara otomatis; justru hak individu harus menjadi pagar utama yang membatasi apa yang boleh dilakukan mayoritas. Memang ada keadaan darurat seperti wabah atau ancaman nyata, tetapi pembatasan harus sempit, sementara, dan diawasi ketat, bukan jadi kebiasaan. Tanpa perlindungan hak yang kuat, “kepentingan masyarakat” mudah berubah menjadi alat menekan orang yang tidak punya kuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
